instructions.summary: Post Twitter thread 6 manually — Zernio free plan exhausted (20/20 posts used)
instructions.detail: The Zernio free plan hit its 20-post/month cap. Thread 6 is a D8 visa → first recibo verde walkthrough — the most conversion-friendly angle yet. Post it manually from @chgmiranda.
instructions.exact_action:
Go to twitter.com, post this thread from @chgmiranda (reply to each tweet to form a thread):

Tweet 1: You just got your D8 visa for Portugal. Congrats!

But within 90 days, you need to issue your first recibo verde (green receipt).

Here is what nobody tells you about the process:

Tweet 2: Step 1: Get your NIF (tax number)

You cannot do anything tax-related without it. This alone can take 2-4 weeks if you do not have a fiscal representative.

The entire process is in Portuguese. No English option.

Tweet 3: Step 2: Register your activity on Portal das Financas

You must pick an activity code (CAE/CIRS) from a dropdown -- in Portuguese.

Pick the wrong one? You could end up in the wrong tax bracket or trigger VAT obligations you did not expect.

Tweet 4: Step 3: Issue your first recibo verde

The form has 12+ fields, all in Portuguese. You need to know:
- Your activity code
- Whether to charge IVA (VAT)
- The correct tax retention rate
- Whether your client is domestic or international

One wrong field = real money consequences.

Tweet 5: The EUR 15,000 trap:

If your cumulative invoices cross EUR 15k in a calendar year, you must register for VAT and charge 23% -- on that invoice.

Most freelancers do not track this until it is too late.

Tweet 6: We are building VerdeDesk -- an English-first interface for this entire workflow.

Issue recibos verdes, track income vs the VAT threshold, get compliance reminders.

No Portuguese required. Launching April 2026.

https://verdedesk.vercel.app
instructions.unblocks: Fresh distribution content on Twitter — our only working autonomous channel
